name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
what_is_it: XSS allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users.
why_matters: Attackers can steal session cookies, deface websites, redirect users to phishing sites, or perform actions on behalf of users.

stored.impact: High - affects all users visiting the page

dom.impact: Medium - client-side only, no server involvement

how_to_verify: 1. Find input fields, URL parameters, or headers that are reflected in the response
3. If an alert box appears when the page loads, XSS is confirmed
4. Try different XSS bypass techniques if filters are in place
remediation_steps: 1. Sanitize all user input - remove HTML tags and script tags
2. Use output encoding when displaying user data (HTML entities)
3. Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to restrict script execution
4. Use secure frameworks that auto-escape by default (React, Angular)
